checks.question: Did the work fully address the issue hints, user notes, and approved plan requirements?

findings.detail: Checked [DataProviderContext.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext.tsx), [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx), and [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx). The revised Step 9.3 says preview FPS should come from `useVideoEditorRuntime()` or similar, but that context only exposes `provider`, `timelineId`, `userId`, and `timelineName`, and the current `EffectCreatorPanel` caller does not pass any fps prop, so this part of the plan is not implementable against the repository as written.

Checked [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts) and [VideoEditorProvider.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.tsx). The revision now correctly models the real provider-local registry plus module-singleton lookup path instead of treating the singleton as the only source of truth, which matches the repository better than the earlier draft.

checks.question: Are the proposed changes technically correct?

findings.detail: Checked [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts#L23) and [index.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx#L78). Step 1.2 proposes calling `replaceEffectRegistry(registry)` synchronously during render, but that mutates module-level state while React is rendering; the current code deliberately keeps that mutation in `useEffect()`, and moving it into render would violate React purity expectations and be brittle under StrictMode or concurrent rendering.

Checked [DataProviderContext.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext.tsx#L4), [useTimelineState.types.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineState.types.ts), and [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx#L433). The preview-timing fix is still technically incomplete because neither the runtime context nor the `EffectCreatorPanel` call chain exposes `config.output.fps`, so the proposed source of truth for preview fps does not exist in the repo today.

Checked [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx#L139) against [EffectLayerSequence.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx#L39). The revised `wrapPreviewEffect` idea does fix a real current mismatch, because preview always passes `effectFrames` from the slider today while timeline continuous layers use `effectFrames={durationInFrames}`.

checks.question: Search for related code that handles the same concept. Is the reported issue a symptom of something broader?

findings.detail: Checked [serialize.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/serialize.ts#L96), [timeline-data.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/timeline-data.ts#L198), [migrate.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/lib/migrate.ts#L165), and [useTimelineCommit.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useTimelineCommit.ts#L260). The broader `customEffects` persistence path is still present, but the revised plan now explicitly treats that as a separate migration follow-up rather than pretending these five fixes will remove it.

Searched all `CustomEffectEditor` references and only found its own definition in [CustomEffectEditor.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/CustomEffectEditor.tsx). Excluding that component from the active migration work matches the actual call graph in the repository.

checks.question: Does the change touch all locations AND supporting infrastructure?

findings.detail: Checked [DynamicEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts#L13) and [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts#L39). Step 1 adds `_notify()` on registration and Step 8 replaces `Promise.all` with sequential `await` loops, but the plan never adds a batch or notification-suppression mechanism, so the supporting infrastructure is incomplete and initialization would trigger one provider re-render per loaded effect.

Checked [index.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/index.tsx#L114) and [EffectLayerSequence.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx#L19). The revised plan still covers both runtime application paths that actually render effects today: clip-level wrapping and effect-layer wrapping.

checks.question: Find the callers of the changed function. What arguments do they actually pass? Does the fix handle all of them?

findings.detail: Checked [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx#L433) and [DataProviderContext.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext.tsx#L4). `EffectCreatorPanel` is only called without an fps prop, and the nearest shared context does not include fps either, so the revised preview-fps step does not line up with the actual caller inputs.

Checked [VisualClip.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/VisualClip.tsx#L223), [TextClip.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/TextClip.tsx#L45), and [EffectLayerSequence.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/compositions/EffectLayerSequence.tsx#L39). The revised plan explicitly preserves the current caller split where clip-level continuous effects omit `effectFrames` but effect layers pass `effectFrames={durationInFrames}`, which is consistent with the repository.

Checked [ParameterControls.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx#L35), [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx#L84), [BulkClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/BulkClipPanel.tsx#L75), and [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx#L185). Reusing `getDefaultValues()` instead of exporting `getFallbackValue()` now matches every current caller that consumes schema defaults.

checks.question: Does the approach match how the codebase solves similar problems?

findings.detail: Checked [ParameterControls.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx#L35), [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx#L84), and [BulkClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/BulkClipPanel.tsx#L75). The revised `validateAndCoerceParams` approach now builds on the existing `getDefaultValues()` helper, which is the same schema-default pattern the rest of the codebase already uses.

Checked [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx#L191). The revised preview plan keeps the compiled preview component in local state and no longer relies on registering preview-only components into the shared effect registry, which is consistent with how the repository currently handles local preview behavior.

Checked the proposed utility placement against [DynamicEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts), [compileEffect.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/compileEffect.tsx), and [ParameterControls.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/ParameterControls.tsx). The revised plan still describes `effects/validateParams.ts` reusing `getDefaultValues()` from a component file, which would make the lower-level effects layer depend on UI code and is a layering inversion relative to the current directory structure.

checks.question: Is there convincing verification for the change?

findings.detail: Checked [DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx), [VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx), and the revised Step 10. The plan still does not add an integration test that drives `useEffectRegistry()` through `VideoEditorProvider` into `lookupEffect()`, and the existing provider test mocks `useEffectRegistry`, so it would not catch a broken registry swap or reactivity bridge.

Checked [types/index.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/types/index.ts#L5) and the revised Steps 6-7. The plan now defines coercion behavior for wrong types and invalid select values, but it still does not propose an automated test that feeds malformed params through runtime application and asserts the normalized output.

Checked [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx#L139) and the revised Step 9. One of the five target fixes is preview fidelity, but the plan still leaves preview behavior to manual smoke testing and does not add any automated coverage around `makePreviewComposition()` or its replacement.

Checked [DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.test.tsx). Adding targeted unit tests for `subscribe()` and `getSnapshot()` there remains consistent with the current test organization.

checks.question: Are the success criteria well-prioritized and verifiable?

findings.detail: Checked the revised success criteria against [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts#L23). The new `must` criterion that `replaceEffectRegistry()` be called synchronously during render is objectively checkable, but it hardcodes a questionable implementation detail into the gate; a safer solution that keeps render pure would fail the plan even if it fixed the user-visible bug.

Checked the revised criterion requiring a test that `useSyncExternalStore` triggers `InnerProvider` updates after async registration. That is a stronger, outcome-oriented gate than the earlier API-shape-only criterion and is verifiable from tests.
Checked the criterion that the three named existing test files pass. That remains a clear yes-or-no validation gate.

flags.concern: video-editor-effects: The revised registry plan moves `replaceEffectRegistry()` into render, which mutates module state during React render and makes the fix depend on an impure render path.
flags.evidence: Current [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts#L30) performs the swap inside `useEffect()`. The revised Step 1.2 explicitly moves that mutation into render to close the first-render gap.

flags.concern: effect-preview-fidelity: The revised preview-fps fix still lacks an implementable source of truth because `EffectCreatorPanel` does not receive fps and `useVideoEditorRuntime()` does not expose it.
flags.evidence: [DataProviderContext.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/DataProviderContext.tsx#L4) exposes only `provider`, `timelineId`, `userId`, and `timelineName`, while [ClipPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/PropertiesPanel/ClipPanel.tsx#L433) renders `EffectCreatorPanel` without any fps prop.

flags.concern: effect-registry-loading: Sequential async registration plus per-register notifications will cause O(n) provider re-renders during effect initialization unless the registry gains a batch or suppress-notify path.
flags.evidence: Current [useEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/hooks/useEffectRegistry.ts#L39) uses `Promise.all`, and current [DynamicEffectRegistry.ts](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/effects/DynamicEffectRegistry.ts#L13) has no batching API. The revised plan combines sequential awaits with `_notify()` on each registration.

flags.concern: video-editor-effects: Recurring debt: the revised plan still does not add an integration test that exercises `useEffectRegistry()` through `VideoEditorProvider` into the global lookup/render path.
flags.evidence: [VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/contexts/VideoEditorProvider.test.tsx#L12) mocks `useEffectRegistry`, so the existing provider test cannot verify the new registry swap or reactivity behavior.

flags.concern: effect-param-validation: Recurring debt: the revised plan still lacks an automated test that proves wrong-type or invalid-option params are coerced at runtime.
flags.evidence: The revised Steps 6-7 define coercion rules, but the listed automated checks only mention existing registry/resource/provider tests and do not include a malformed-param case through runtime effect application.

flags.concern: effect-preview-fidelity: Recurring debt: preview fidelity remains untested in automation even though it is one of the five target architectural fixes.
flags.evidence: [EffectCreatorPanel.tsx](/Users/peteromalley/Documents/reigh-workspace/reigh-app/src/tools/video-editor/components/EffectCreatorPanel.tsx#L139) contains the preview composition logic, but the revised plan still leaves preview validation to manual smoke testing only.
